title: Storage, Filesystems, Archives, and Backups · Command Brief: Backup Restore Proof
summary: Respond to low disk space and protect service data without confusing filesystems, mounts, archives, snapshots, and verified backups. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux backup restore proof increment in a storage map and restore-tested backup plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define backup scope, consistency, retention, integrity, isolation, and restore testing as separate controls.
Create, inspect, extract, and verify archives while protecting path traversal, ownership, permissions, and compression expectations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Backup Restore Proof
steps.instruction:
theory case — Filesystem / is 92% full with 400 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Backup Restore Proof contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define backup scope, consistency, retention, integrity, isolation, and restore testing as separate controls.

content.text: Filesystem / is 92% full with 400 free inodes while a deleted-open log consumes 1.4 GiB. Build an archive and restore proof without formatting, mounting, or deleting host data.

content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Backup Restore Proof condition decided your prediction.

content.value: GNU Bash Reference Manual — Controls Bash parsing, expansion, quoting, redirection, pipelines, startup, job control, scripting, and error behavior.

options.text: A successful backup command proves the data can be restored. This does not resolve predict backup restore proof.

For predict backup restore proof, apply Backup Restore Proof without its stated preconditions, omit the a storage map and restore-tested backup plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Backup Restore Proof for a storage map and restore-tested backup plan.
Define backup scope, consistency, retention, integrity, isolation, and restore testing as separate controls. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to Backup Restore Proof while producing a storage map and restore-tested backup plan.
